narratives.full_description:
Together with Invisible Friends Animation School, we will go on a field expedition to the abandoned village near Goris to create an experimental animated short film.

Animation Laboratory is a collective filmmaking lab developed by Invisible Friends Animation School in collaboration with hosq  Foundation. The project brings together participants from different disciplines to create an experimental animated short film using pixelation (stop-motion with real people). 
 
 The core of the lab is a 5-day field expedition to the abandoned village of Hin-Khot in Armenia, where the location becomes both a setting and a co-author of the film. Working in a non-hierarchical format, participants contribute across all stages — from concept to filming and sound — shaping the project together through collaboration. 
 
 hosq  joins the project by developing the film’s sound world and leading its audio production, involving sound curators, artists, and composers from its community.

narratives.engagement_narrative:
hosq  x Invisible Friends — Animation Laboratory in Hin-Khot village 
 (Inner Open Call) 
 
 At the beginning of May, together with Invisible Friends Animation School , we will go on a field expedition to the abandoned village near Goris to create an experimental animated short film. 
 
 We’re excited to invite hosq  sound artists/composers to join us and help build the sound world for a collective film. 
 
 Where : the abandoned village Hin‑Khot  (near Goris). 
 When : a 5-day filming expedition between May 3–13. 
 
 Topic : Invisible presences — a story about a place where people once lived and left, but their dreams and promises stayed. These dreams continue to exist in the village, waiting. And when someone arrives who is still capable of dreaming, they try to reach out, to connect, to be fulfilled. The film will explore dreams as living entities and the rituals that surround them. 
 
 This is gonna be a collective film created using pixelation technique (stop-motion with real people), where the space of an abandoned village becomes a character and a co-author. Since this is a collective filmmaking process, the topic idea remains open — shaped, expanded, and transformed together with you into the final film. 
 
 You can watch last year's lab film here . 
 
 What we offer: 
 learning through practice — working on a real film and sound collection (something meaningful to include in your portfolio 😉) 
 exchange and collaboration with participants from different disciplines 
 guidance and support from curators 
 shared experience of intensive lab process in a beautiful countryside location — fresh air, slow rhythm, and a chance to reset and recharge 
 
 Travel and living costs are covered by hosq . 
 Field conditions : tent camping and shared meals — like being at summer camp 🙃

narratives.site_specific_rationale_narrative:
Create a sound concept: decide what this world “sounds like” (close/intimate vs wide/empty, warm vs harsh, human vs not‑human) 
 Help shape music if needed: minimal motifs, drones, pulses, or silence — whatever supports the film’s rhythm (not “background music”) 
 
 Who this is for  This is for you if you: [b] 
 You enjoy field recording + sound design + composition (any mix is great) 
 You’re an emerging artist who is eager to try new mediums and join our broadened artists' community 
 You’re comfortable working with a group where ideas change fast, and decisions are made together 
 Extra welcome if you also like crossing into other roles on set (animation/performance/acting/set design/costume design/camera) 
 
 To apply, please answer:
